flat_unirig_westlake.role: Westlake-only development comparison; do not replace with another server's checkpoint

verified_fixed_causes: the final rootless-v3 target contains no synthetic root and no generated tail token
the query mesh and target skeleton use the same random query pose and query-bbox coordinate system
global RT is removed from the current training input and is not the active data hypothesis
the current loader returns 1024 ordered query-aligned surface anchors
verified_open_facts: the flat UniRig baseline can generate useful skeletons but still has hitmax and topology-template failures
motion affects prediction and is not completely ignored, but its contribution is not a stable articulation mechanism
the current motion condition mixes static appearance, action magnitude, local relative motion, and learned feature drift
the final skeleton loss does not explicitly require the motion encoder to recover same-segment relations or skin boundaries
low observed motion is unknown evidence rather than a negative structural label
early condition-to-joint errors can be amplified when the autoregressive self-prefix later dominates the condition
unknowns: whether topology-local motion evidence improves child and branch generation without degrading root grounding or normal controls
whether vertex-to-query aggregation preserves the held-out edge signal at the model's 1024 FPS anchors
whether key=static-query and value=motion-only decoder attention receives nonzero gradients and remains stable during free generation
next_required_result: Implement an isolated topology-local motion-evidence encoder and decoder evidence-attention path on the flat UniRig route. Verify exact zero-motion behavior, query/target/reference alignment, finite nonzero gradients, correct-versus-corrupted motion intervention, bounded memory/runtime, teacher-forcing consistency, and small free-generation visualizations before enabling any formal training submission.

active_operation.name: local_motion_evidence_decoder_preflight_20260724
active_operation.motion_relation: topology-edge relative length RMS aggregated to vertices and then to the existing 1024 query references
active_operation.attention_contract: decoder query=skeleton prefix, key=aligned static query token, value=motion-only evidence
active_operation.formal_resources: two A100-80GB in an allowed Westlake resource group
active_operation.acceptance_test: exact zero evidence; aligned 1024-token values; nonzero gradients; correct motion affects later joints more than corrupted motion; normal root behavior; bounded runtime/memory; teacher-forcing and free-generation visual checks

last_operation.name: local_relative_motion_evidence_preflight_20260724
last_operation.conclusion: On 4096 train and all 857 asset-disjoint valid assets, correct local edge motion reached global AUROC 0.7499 and per-asset median 0.8162; within-asset corruption fell to median 0.5024 and zero motion to 0.5. Motion below q90 RMS 1e-3 was near-unknown. Raw RMS matched the diagnostic MLP, so the MLP is not retained as the method.
